problem: Why does acute pain become chronic in some people — what neural change occurs?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: The key to Why does acute pain become chronic in some people — what neural change occurs? is Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations — specifically Optogenetic control of — which would help 50,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 31 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
